codeReview.summary: The fix does resolve two of the three original blockers in the shipped UI: the Button live variant is no longer green-filled, and the global body text default is now #a0a0a0. It also remaps most prior `text-accent`/`bg-accent` UI surfaces away from blue. However, the token purge is still incomplete: `globals.css` continues to ship blue and green general-purpose UI effect tokens outside the allowed atmospheric-gradient/status-dot contexts, and the baseline test suite still codifies those stale accent utilities instead of guarding the stricter rule.

issues.description: The stylesheet still defines forbidden non-atmospheric/non-dot accent utilities after the fix: `--border-accent: rgba(69, 94, 255, 0.25)` at line 57 and `--color-border-accent: var(--border-accent)` at line 116 keep #455eff available as a general UI border token, while `.glow-accent` at lines 204-207 and `--glow-green` / `.glow-green` at lines 61 and 211-214 keep blue and green glow utilities for generic UI highlighting. The re-run brief was explicit that `#455eff` must only appear in atmospheric gradients and `#00ff00` must only appear as status dots, so the shipped token system still misses the strict purge even though the most visible component usages were remapped.

The verification suite still locks in the stale accent model instead of the stricter purge. The baseline tests explicitly require an accent border token at line 32 and require blue glow usage (`rgba(69, 94, 255`) at lines 54-56. That means the tests currently reinforce the leftover forbidden utilities in `globals.css` rather than protecting the re-run contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared state is internally inconsistent on the blue-accent rule. Mission AGENTS says `#455eff` is for atmospheric gradients only and green is for status indicators only, but the committed tradebetter comparison artifact still documents blue accent borders/focus states and even 'electric-blue accent CTAs'. That stale artifact likely contributes to workers and tests preserving forbidden accent utilities during token-fix work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission rule: `/Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/AGENTS.md:157-160`. Conflicting artifact: `/Users/test/vision/.factory/library/tradebetter-comparison-evidence.md:42`, `:58`, `:96`, `:112`, and `:148` still describe `--border-accent: #455eff40`, blue focus borders, and electric-blue accent CTAs.

summary: Fail. The fix clears the original green CTA fill and body-text-default issues and remaps most blue UI accent usages to white, but it does not fully satisfy the stricter token-purge contract because `globals.css` still ships blue border/glow utilities and green glow utilities outside atmospheric-gradient/status-dot contexts. I also noted a shared-state conflict: the committed tradebetter comparison artifact still documents blue accent borders/CTAs, which conflicts with the mission’s stricter color rules.
